Off Calendar Oct-21-2019 Continued To Nov-18-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-11-295236 Conservatorship of Yelena Frumkin and
PTR-14-297419 The Yelena Frumkin Special Needs Trust.

Appearances: Mila Lvovich for Sulamite Rapoport (Conservator/Trustee).

The Court went over a list of items that were to be filed prior to this hearing. Ms. Lvovich stated that everything was filed on Thursday, that she sent photos of the Conservatee's living condition to the Court Investigator on October 11th, that her Client does not want the photos to be filed as part of the conservatorship file, that her Client is adamant about not disclosing the photos and that she is representing to the Court that the photos that were e-mailed to the Court Investigator on October 11th are of Ms. Frumkin's home. The Fourth Account and Report of Conservator, etc. and the Third Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are continued to November 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on the conservatee's living situation currently set on the conservatorship calendar for October 24, 2019 is also being continued to November 18, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to be heard on the probate morning calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-09-2019 Continued To Oct-21-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-11-295236 Conservatorship of Yelena Frumkin and
PTR-14-297419 The Yelena Frumkin Special Needs Trust.

Appearances: Milla Lvovich for and with Sulamite Rapoport.

The Court asked for clarification on whether or not the Conservatee is receiving in-home support services ("IHSS"). Ms. Lvovich stated that her Client was confused, that there is a Woman that gets paid from IHSS for 70 hours a month, that Yelena (Conservatee) does not know this Woman is getting paid or she would refuse the services/help and that she can amend the schedules to reflect the burial as an asset. Ms. Lvovich also stated that in 2013 disbursements to the Conservatee in the amount of $800.00 a month was approved, that the costs of living has increased, that her Client feels that if the Daughter has more freedom and is allowed to manage her money it makes the Daughter feel better and is good for her, and that the condominium is rented out and provides enough for the disbursement. The Court stated that the Court ordered $800.00 and that it should remain until a petition is filed to change that and do allocation between the trust and the conservatorship. The Court also reminded Counsel that it needs a change of address. Ms. Lvovich stated that the Conservatee's living situatuion is fine, that the Conservatee does not want anyone to come in to her home and that she can provide photos. The Third Account and Report of Trustee, etc. and the Fourth Account and Report of Conservator, etc. are continued to October 21, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for filing of an amendment that addresses the allowance and the funeral expenses. Status on the Conservatee's living situation is set for October 10, 2019 at 9:30 a.m., on the conservatorship calendar in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Ms. Lvovich reported that checks were sent to the personal representative of Ms. Barrese's estate for payment of fees. The Court stated that he wanted written confirmation that the fees were received. He inquired whether or not the conservator was waiving fees. Ms. Lvovich confirmed that the Conservator was waiving fees, and would provide the Court documentation regarding the delivery of checks to the representative of Ms. Barrese's estate. The matter was taken under submission pending receipt of proof of payment.

Off Calendar Nov-06-2017 Continued To Jan-08-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were heard: PCN-11-295236 Conservatorship of Yelena Frumkin and
PTR-14-297419 The Yelena Frumkin Special Needs Trust.

Appearances: Mila Lvovich for Sulamite Rapoport (Conservator/Trustee).

Ms. Lvovich stated that the annual amount for two bonds is $220.00, that the premium on the $126,000.00 bond is $120.00 per year, that her Client is elderly and that it is easier for her Client to keep track of one bond. Ms. Lvovich also stated that her Client had a lot of grievances regarding Ms. Barrese's services, that at Ms. Barrese's fee hearing she was told by Judge Cheng that they could raise the issue of reducing fees at a later time, that it was a verbal request and that there was no motion. The Court stated that it will not re-open this issue, that it is a binding order, that it is too late to file a motion and that it will not pay any fees until old fees have been paid. The Third Account and Report of Conservator, etc. and Second Account and Report of Trustee, etc. are continued to January 8, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of Ms. Barrese's fees.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Chu. Not reported.

The Court stated that this is a request to eliminate or reduce the bond and that it will reduce the bond but not eliminate it and explained why. Ms. Lvovich stated that the conservator is the mother of the conservatee. The Court stated it that is sure she is a wonderful conservator and will reduce the bond to $70,000.00 but will not eliminate it. Off Calendar May-12-2015 Continued To Jun-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard together: PCN-11-295236 Conservatorship of Yelena Frumkin and PTR-14-297419 Yelena Frumkin Special Needs Trust.
Appearances: Kendal Fletcher for (conservator & Trustee) Sulamite Rapaport.

The Court noted that a petition had been filed in the conservatorship but not in the trust. Ms. Fletcher reported that they had intended to file in both cases, that they were unable to get the trust petition together in time, that there are concerns regarding the previous counsel for the trustee, that they have had to go through documents with her, that they are close to completing the petition and need a 1 month continuance. The status of filing an accounting in case PTR-14-297419 was continued to June 9, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The status of filing an accounting in case PCN-11-295236 was taken off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)

Petition For Order For Attorney Fees (Pc 1472) And For Terminating Representation As Counsel For Trustee
Off Calendar Apr-23-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: The following cases were heard together: PCN-11-295236 Conservatorship of Yelena Frumkin & PTR-14-297419 The Yelena Frumkin Special Needs Trust.
Appearances: Milla Lvovich for (conservator) Sulamite Rapaport. Irene Barrese (petitioner) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Barrese stated that she made a mistake, that the 2 daughters were direct beneficiaries and that the sister and mother should have been mentioned. Ms. Lvovich stated that the issue of fees should be taken up with the accounting petition scheduled for May 12, 2015, that she will be more prepared to address the fees at that time and that her client is not happy with the amount of the fees. Ms. Barrese stated that the fee agreement was set with her former client, that the fees have been lowered at her request and that they said they could and would pay. Ms. Lvovich stated that they are working on completing the accounting, that her clients have agreed to make installment payments to Ms. Barrese, they feel the fees should be reduced and that she will be more capable of addressing this at the May hearing. Ms. Barrese stated that the issue should have been brought up earlier. The Petition by Attorney for Order of Discharge as Conservator's attorney and for attorney fees (case PCN-11-295236) and Petition for Order for attorney fees and for Terminating Representation as Counsel for Trustee (case PTR-14-297419 are taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
